memberdef.definition: using faiss::simd_result_handlers::ReservoirHandler< C, with_id_map, SL >::T = typename C::T
memberdef.name: T

memberdef.definition: using faiss::simd_result_handlers::ReservoirHandler< C, with_id_map, SL >::TI = typename C::TI
memberdef.name: TI

memberdef.definition: using faiss::simd_result_handlers::ReservoirHandler< C, with_id_map, SL >::RHC = ResultHandlerCompare<C, with_id_map, SL>
memberdef.name: RHC

briefdescription.para: these fields are used mainly for the IVF variants (with_id_map=true)

memberdef.argsstring: (size_t nq, size_t ntotal, size_t k, size_t cap, float *dis, int64_t *ids, const IDSelector *sel_in)
memberdef.name: ReservoirHandler

memberdef.argsstring: (size_t q, size_t b, simd16uint16 d0, simd16uint16 d1) final
memberdef.name: handle

memberdef.argsstring: (uint16_t thr, size_t b, simd16uint16 d0, simd16uint16 d1)
memberdef.name: get_lt_mask

detaileddescription.para: return binary mask of elements below thr in (d0, d1) inverse_test returns elements above
Set context information for handlers that need additional data
This method can be overridden by handlers that need list numbers and probe mappings (e.g., RaBitQ handlers). Base implementation does nothing since most handlers don't need this context.

parameterdescription.para: current inverted list number being processed
mapping from local query index to probe index

detaileddescription.para: Simple top-N implementation using a reservoir.
Results are stored when they are below the threshold until the capacity is reached. Then a partition sort is used to update the threshold. Handler built from several ReservoirTopN (one per query)
